Yield Curve Event Reaction Tracker

Generated 2026-06-16 10:08 UTC UTC

Which scheduled US releases (CPI, the jobs report, FOMC decisions) move the Treasury curve, by how much, and at which maturities. Use the dropdown to switch between rate regimes and recent periods. Short windows hold only a few releases, so their reaction multiples are noisy; a caption flags any window where that applies.

Full history (2018–today)

2018-01-01 to 2026-06-16 · 2,112 trading days

Between 2018-01-01 and 2026-06-16 this window covers 2,112 US Treasury trading days. On CPI release days the 2-year yield moved 1.6x its typical daily range; the jobs report (NFP) moved it 1.9x, and FOMC decision days 1.6x. At the long end the 30-year moved 0.9x on FOMC days. The single largest reaction was on NFP days at the 2y point — 1.9x a normal day's move. A reaction multiple above 1.0x means the release reliably moves that part of the curve more than an average session; multiples are measured against a baseline of 1,848 non-event (NORMAL) days.

|Δ| = absolute daily change in bp. Reaction multiple = mean |Δ| on that event type ÷ mean |Δ| on NORMAL days. MULTI days (more than one release) are excluded from single-event rows.

2019 mid-cycle cuts

2019-01-01 to 2019-12-31 · 250 trading days

Small sample (CPI n=11, FOMC n=7): reaction multiples here are noisy, interpret with caution.

Between 2019-01-01 and 2019-12-31 this window covers 250 US Treasury trading days. On CPI release days the 2-year yield moved 1.1x its typical daily range; the jobs report (NFP) moved it 1.2x, and FOMC decision days 1.6x. At the long end the 30-year moved 1.0x on FOMC days. The single largest reaction was on FOMC days at the 2y point — 1.6x a normal day's move. A reaction multiple above 1.0x means the release reliably moves that part of the curve more than an average session; multiples are measured against a baseline of 219 non-event (NORMAL) days.

2020 COVID shock

2020-01-01 to 2020-12-31 · 251 trading days

Small sample (CPI n=10, FOMC n=6): reaction multiples here are noisy, interpret with caution.

Between 2020-01-01 and 2020-12-31 this window covers 251 US Treasury trading days. On CPI release days the 2-year yield moved 0.4x its typical daily range; the jobs report (NFP) moved it 1.2x, and FOMC decision days 0.6x. At the long end the 30-year moved 0.6x on FOMC days. The single largest reaction was on NFP days at the 30y point — 1.8x a normal day's move. A reaction multiple above 1.0x means the release reliably moves that part of the curve more than an average session; multiples are measured against a baseline of 222 non-event (NORMAL) days.

2022–23 hiking cycle

2022-01-01 to 2023-12-31 · 499 trading days

Between 2022-01-01 and 2023-12-31 this window covers 499 US Treasury trading days. On CPI release days the 2-year yield moved 2.0x its typical daily range; the jobs report (NFP) moved it 1.9x, and FOMC decision days 1.9x. At the long end the 30-year moved 0.9x on FOMC days. The single largest reaction was on CPI days at the 2y point — 2.0x a normal day's move. A reaction multiple above 1.0x means the release reliably moves that part of the curve more than an average session; multiples are measured against a baseline of 435 non-event (NORMAL) days.

The single largest reaction was on NFP days at the 2y point — 2.2x a normal day's move.
